---
title: "Project"
author: "Skilyn Leon"
format: pdf
toc: true
editor: visual
---

## The network (G)

![Ciel network plot](plot.png){fig-align="center" width="581"}

## Table of G (ranked)

| Node | Clustering Coefficient | Degree Centrality (Rank) | Betweenness Centrality | Closeness Centrality |
|----|:---|----|----|----|
| 1 | 0.05 (6) | 3/8 (3) | 0.6 (2) | 0.6 (1) |
| 2 | 0.07 (5) | 0.46 (1) | 0.6 (1) | 0.6 (1) |
| 3 | 1 (1) | 0.08 (6) | 0 (9) | 0.4 (7) |
| 4 | 1/6 (4) | 1/6 (4) | 0.16 (4) | 0.41 (8) |
| 5 | 0 (7) | 0.04 (7) | 0 (9) | 0.3 (12) |
| 6 | 0 (7) | 0.04 (7) | 0 (9) | 0.38 (9) |
| 7 | 1/3 (2) | 1/6 (4) | 0.04 (7) | 0.44 (3) |
| 8 | 1/3 (2) | 1/8 (5) | 0.2 (8) | 0.38 (9) |
| 9 | 1 (1) | 0.08 (6) | 0 (9) | 0.33 (10) |
| 10 | 1/3 (2) | 1/6 (4) | 0.06 (6) | 0.44 (3) |
| 11 | 1/3 (2) | 1/6 (4) | 0.09 (5) | 0.42 (4) |
| 12 | 0 (7) | 0.04 (7) | 0 (9) | 0.38 (9) |
| 13 | 0.2 (3) | 0.2 (2) | 0.12 (3) | 4/9 (2) |
| 14 | 0 (7) | 0.04 (7) | 0 (9) | 0.38 (9) |
| 15 | 0 (7) | 0.04 (7) | 0 (9) | 0.3 (11) |
| 16 | 0 (7) | 0.04 (7) | 0 (9) | 0.38 (9) |
| 17 | 1 (1) | 0.08 (6) | 0 (9) | 0.4 (7) |
| 18 | 0 (7) | 0.04 (7) | 0 (9) | 0.38 (9) |
| 19 | 0 (7) | 0.04 (7) | 0 (9) | 0.38 (9) |
| 20 | 0 (7) | 0.04 (7) | 0 (9) | 0.38 (9) |
| 21 | 0 (7) | 0.04 (7) | 0 (9) | 0.38 (9) |
| 22 | 1 (1) | 0.08 (6) | 0 (9) | 0.41 (5) |
| 23 | 0 (7) | 0.04 (7) | 0 (9) | 0.3 (12) |
| 24 | 1 (1) | 0.08 (6) | 0 (9) | 0.4 (6) |
| 25 | 0 (7) | 0.04 (7) | 0 (9) | 0.38 (9) |

![](deg_vs_bet.png)

![](dev.png){fig-align="center"}

## Analysis

The calculated density of the network is 0.11, which is quite low. This suggests that the network is sparse, meaning most nodes do not have direct connections with many others. Instead, the network relies on key intermediaries for communication and coordination. Degree Centrality identifies the most connected nodes, the ones with the highest number of direct connections. Betweenness Centrality reveals nodes that act as bridges or intermediaries. Closeness Centrality highlights nodes that can quickly reach others in the network. Clustering Coefficient measures how connected a node’s neighbors are to each other, indicating local connectivity. With this in mind, node 2 is likely the ring leader with rank 1 in Degree, Betweenness, and Closeness. Node 1 is likely the second in command (lieutenant) since its rank close second to node 2. Node 7 commuates with node 2 the most other than node 1. Drug Mules are nodes with medium-high connectivity but low clustering (10, 7, 24). Complementary Segment are nodes 8, 9, 16, 21, 23 with strong internal cliques but not as connected to the larger network. In order to dismantle the network removing nodes 1, 2, 13, 7 would disrupt the major connections.

## Appendix

```{python}
#run these first
import numpy as np
import networkx as nx
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
```

```{python}
G = nx.Graph()
graph_edges = [
    (1, 2), (1, 3), (1, 4),(1,16),(1,6),(1,20),(1,18),(1,13),(1,22),
    (2, 17), (2, 7),(2, 19),(2,21),(2,12),(2,25),(2,14),(2,10),(2,11),(2,24),
    (3, 4),
    (4,5),(4,15),
    (7, 17), (7, 13),(7,10),
    (8,9), (8, 10),(8,13),
    (9, 13), 
    (10, 11),
    (11, 24), (11, 23),
    (13,22)
]
G.add_edges_from(graph_edges)
```

## Adjacency matrix of G

```{python}
#creating adjancency matrix
adjacency_matrix = nx.to_numpy_array(G, nodelist=sorted(G.nodes()), dtype=int)
print("Symmetric Adjacency Matrix:")
print(adjacency_matrix)
```

```{python}
#recreating ciel network
plt.figure(figsize = (7, 7))
nx.draw(G, with_labels=True, node_color='purple', node_size=700, font_size=10, font_color='white', font_weight='bold', edge_color='gray')
plt.title("Ciel Network Visualization")
plt.show()
```

```{python}
#Calculating the degree centrality, betweenness centrality, clustering coefficient, closeness centrality, density and global/mean clustering coefficient
degree_centrality = nx.degree_centrality(G)
betweenness_centrality = nx.betweenness_centrality(G)
closeness_centrality = nx.closeness_centrality(G)
clustering_coefficient = nx.clustering(G)
density = nx.density(G)
avg_clustering = nx.average_clustering(G)
#making a data frame to show the values 
metrics_df = pd.DataFrame({
    'Node': sorted(G.nodes()),
    'Degree Centrality': [degree_centrality[node] for node in sorted(G.nodes())],
    'Betweenness Centrality': [betweenness_centrality[node] for node in sorted(G.nodes())],
    'Closeness Centrality': [closeness_centrality[node] for node in sorted(G.nodes())],
    'Clustering Coefficient': [clustering_coefficient[node] for node in sorted(G.nodes())],
    'avg_clustering': avg_clustering,
    'Density': density
})
print(metrics_df)
```

```{python}
#creating a plot for show Degree centrality vs Betweenness Centrality
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
plt.scatter(degree_centrality.values(), betweenness_centrality.values(), color='lavender', edgecolors='black', s=100, alpha=0.7)

for node in G.nodes():
    plt.text(degree_centrality[node], betweenness_centrality[node], str(node), fontsize=10.5)

avg_degree = np.mean(list(degree_centrality.values()))
avg_betweenness = np.mean(list(betweenness_centrality.values()))

plt.axvline(avg_degree, color='c', linestyle='dashed', label=f'Avg Degree Centrality ({avg_degree:.2f})')
plt.axhline(avg_betweenness, color='deeppink', linestyle='dashed', label=f'Avg Betweenness Centrality ({avg_betweenness:.2f})')

plt.xlabel("Degree Centrality")
plt.ylabel("Betweenness Centrality")
plt.title("Degree Centrality vs Betweenness Centrality")
plt.legend()
plt.grid(True)

plt.show()
```

```{python}
#Finding the vaules 
avg_closeness = np.mean(list(closeness_centrality.values()))
betweenness_deviation = {node: value - avg_betweenness for node, value in betweenness_centrality.items()}
degree_deviation = {node: value - avg_degree for node, value in degree_centrality.items()}
closeness_deviation = {node: value - avg_closeness for node, value in closeness_centrality.items()}
#creating a data frame to show values 
dataframe = pd.DataFrame({
    'Node': sorted(G.nodes()),
    'Betweenness Deviation': [betweenness_deviation[node] for node in sorted(G.nodes())],
    'Degree Deviation': [degree_deviation[node] for node in sorted(G.nodes())],
    'Closeness Deviation': [closeness_deviation[node] for node in sorted(G.nodes())]
})
print(dataframe)

plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))

# Plot Betweenness Deviation
plt.plot(list(betweenness_deviation.keys()), list(betweenness_deviation.values()), label='Betweenness Deviation', marker='o', color='blue')
for node, value in betweenness_deviation.items():
    plt.text(node, value, str(node), fontsize=8, ha='right')

# Plot Degree Deviation
plt.plot(list(degree_deviation.keys()), list(degree_deviation.values()), label='Degree Deviation', marker='s', color='blueviolet')
for node, value in degree_deviation.items():
    plt.text(node, value, str(node), fontsize=8, ha='right')

# Plot Closeness Deviation
plt.plot(list(closeness_deviation.keys()), list(closeness_deviation.values()), label='Closeness Deviation', marker='^', color='magenta')
for node, value in closeness_deviation.items():
    plt.text(node, value, str(node), fontsize=8, ha='right')

plt.xlabel('Nodes')
plt.ylabel('Deviation from Average')
plt.title('Centrality Measure Deviations')
plt.legend()
plt.grid(True)
plt.show()

```
